what_it_does: React implementation of the Input form-field component using React.createElement (aliased h). Renders one of .cv-input/.cv-textarea/.cv-select based on the as prop, sets aria-invalid when error is present, and — if any of label/hint/error are provided — wraps the control in a .cv-field div with a .cv-label and a .cv-hint span (the hint switches to error text and .cv-hint--error styling when error is set).
observations: No JSX; uses const h = React.createElement and a /* global React */ comment (consumes a global React).
Control class chosen by as: textarea→cv-textarea, select→cv-select, else cv-input; appends caller className.
Bare-control shortcut: if label, hint, and error are all null, returns the control element alone.
aria-invalid set to 'true' only when error is truthy, else undefined.
When wrapped, the .cv-hint span shows error text if present, otherwise hint; error adds the cv-hint--error class.
Header comment notes the gold focus ring and underlying classes come from tokens/controls.css and 'the source's composer'.
standouts: Single error/hint span that prefers error over hint, keeping one message slot.
Token-class-only styling (no inline styles) — purely a class wrapper over CSS-defined controls.
conventions: Class-driven styling referencing design-system tokens/classes (no raw values) — matches the single-source directive.
createElement instead of JSX, suggesting a no-build / bundle-friendly module.
Polymorphic 'as' element selection matching the .d.ts contract.
notes: The Input field primitive: a class-wrapper over .cv-input/.cv-textarea/.cv-select that optionally builds the full .cv-field label/hint/error stack and sets aria-invalid.
questions: Where are .cv-input/.cv-textarea/.cv-select/.cv-field/.cv-hint defined (tokens/controls.css, referenced not shown here)?
Is the reliance on a global React (not imported) consistent with how this module is bundled?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — header comment describes a token-class wrapper with an optional .cv-field stack; the code implements exactly that and matches its .d.ts.
apparent_themes: Class-as-styling single source (CSS tokens carry the look)
intention_signals: Aims to keep the form field as a minimal class-wrapping React layer with no styling literals of its own, deferring all appearance to the design-system control classes.
contribution: Contributes the runtime that maps Input props to the correct control class and conditionally assembles the .cv-field label/hint/error stack with error-priority messaging and aria-invalid.
summary_for_embedding: React Input component built with React.createElement that renders .cv-input/.cv-textarea/.cv-select by the as prop, sets aria-invalid on error, and optionally wraps the control in a .cv-field stack with label and an error-or-hint span.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a styling-free, class-driven form-field wrapper that defers all appearance to design-system control classes.
